created_by.name: Pranav Rajpurkar
created_by.affiliation: Stanford University, Department of Computer Science

signature.note: No canonical artifact hash has been published by the dataset authors. The HuggingFace-hosted Parquet files would need to be hashed at a specific commit to produce a stable digest.

lineage.description: English Wikipedia articles (October 2015 snapshot) were filtered to 536 articles covering a range of topics, then converted from raw MediaWiki markup to plain text.

inputs.name: English Wikipedia dump
inputs.note: The exact dump date and file used are not documented in the SQuAD paper or dataset card.

operations: Selected 536 Wikipedia articles across diverse topics
Converted MediaWiki markup to plain text, stripping citations, tables, and infoboxes
Segmented articles into paragraphs for use as reading passages
lineage.note: Intermediate passage corpus was never published as a standalone artifact.

lineage.description: Crowdworkers on Amazon Mechanical Turk were shown Wikipedia passages and asked to write question-answer pairs where the answer is a contiguous span from the passage.
lineage.annotation_methodology: Crowdworkers read a passage and authored questions whose answers are extractive spans. Each passage was shown to multiple workers to increase coverage. Unanswerability was not a criterion in v1.0 (added in SQuAD 2.0).
operations: Crowdworkers authored question-answer pairs against each passage
Answers were constrained to be exact character spans from the passage
Multiple workers annotated each passage independently
lineage.note: Raw MTurk annotation output was never published. Annotator count and inter-annotator agreement metrics are not documented in the dataset card.

lineage.description: Annotated question-answer pairs were validated, deduplicated, and formatted into the final SQuAD JSON structure, then converted to Parquet for distribution on HuggingFace.
operations: Validated that all answer spans are exact substrings of the corresponding passage
Serialized to SQuAD JSON format (context, question, answers with answer_start offsets)
Split into train (87,599 examples) and validation (10,570 examples)
Converted to Parquet for HuggingFace Hub distribution
lineage.note: Processing scripts on GitHub reflect the HuggingFace Datasets repackaging, not the original Stanford processing pipeline.

attestation_gaps.note: No third-party bias audit has been conducted or signed against a specific dataset version. Known bias: Wikipedia over-represents certain topics, demographics, and writing styles.
No attestation for EU AI Act, CRA, or equivalent regulatory compliance exists for this dataset.
No independent quality certification exists. The paper reports human-level F1 of 86.8 on validation, but this is a model benchmark, not a dataset quality attestation.
